---
title: "Program Decomposition Part 2 (class slides)"
format: revealjs
slide-number: true
df-print: kable
---


# CSC -- More on Decomposition

## Program decomposition -- Review

-   Program decomposition refers to the process of breaking down a complex program into smaller, more manageable, and understandable parts or components.

-   It avoids unnecessary common code and improves the structural quality of code.


## Decompose this function into at least 2 functions

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
def main():
    birthday = ""
    fav_num = ""
    counter = 0
    count_dict = {}
    while not birthday.isnumeric():
        birthday = input("Enter the day of your birthday\n")
    while not fav_num.isnumeric():
        fav_num = input("Enter your favorite number\n")
    for i in range(int(birthday) + 1):
        if int(birthday) % i == 0:
            counter += 1
    count_dict["birthday"] = counter
    counter = 0
    for i in range(int(fav_num) + 1):
        if int(fav_num) % i == 0:
            counter += 1
    count_dict["favorite number"] = counter
```

## Decomposition example solution

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
def count_divisible(message):
    num = ""
    while not num.isnumeric():
        num = input("Enter " + message + "\n")
    counter = 0
    for i in range (int(num) + 1):
        if int(num) % i == 0:
            counter += 1
    return counter

def main():
    count_dict = {}
    birthday_counter = count_divisible("the day of your birthday")
    fav_num_counter = count_divisible("your favorite number")
    count_dict["birthday"] = birthday_counter
    count_dict["favorite number"] = fav_num_counter
```

## Decompose just from a description

-   Create code that: asks the user for two strings, checking that strings have only vowels. Keep asking the user to enter the string again if their string had a non-vowel or was empty. Then, count the number of "ae" in each string. In main(), print out the string with the most instances of "ae"

## Decompose Possible Solution

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
def check_string(s_to_check):
    if s_to_check == "":
        return False
    for i in s_to_check:
        if i not in "aeiou":
            return False
    return True

def get_string():
    a_string = ""
    if not check_string(a_string):
        a_string = input("Enter a string with only vowels\n")
    return a_string

def count_string(s_to_count):
    counter = 0
    for i in range(len(s_to_count) - 1):
        if s_to_count[i] + s_to_count[i+1] == "ae":
            counter += 1
    return counter

def main():
    string_1 = get_string()
    string_2 = get_string()
    string_1_count = count_string(string_1)
    string_2_count = count_string(string_2)
    if string_1_count > string_2_count:
        print(string_1)
    else:
        print(string_2)
```
